summary_one_line: Winner must feed bytes that match a recognized SQL token at the tokenizer character-class CMP, gated by value_profile's prefix-distance feedback.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input bytes at the tokenizer cursor that satisfy the true-side character-class / keyword comparison at sqlite3GetToken line 159623, e.g. an identifier/keyword character run such as 'SELECT', 'CEXT', or a numeric-operator expression, rather than arbitrary punctuation/whitespace bytes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile-stored winner seeds carry recognizable SQL token fragments ('SELECT 11-1133=13...', 'CEXT 1', long uppercase-letter runs) and drive the true side (L159623 W=T:4 vs L=T:0); the winner then reaches deep expression-resolution code (walkExpr, resolveExprStep) the loser never touches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser-blocking seeds are scattered punctuation/whitespace-heavy bytes ('T 1; c BINARY', ';$..C..C', leading ' ;.....') that never match the token-class comparison, so L159623 stays false (T:0 F:78) and downstream expression walkers have zero invocations.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket): each tokenizer character/keyword CMP that gets closer to matching adds a new bucket and the near-miss input is preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient that ratchets bytes toward a valid token; naive has only edge counters and no such gradient, so it must hit the exact token by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair at this branch is value_profile beating naive on the value_profile technique axis.
value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in 9 of 10.
The blocker is the true side of a branch inside the SQL tokenizer sqlite3GetToken.
Winner seeds contain recognizable SQL token text including SELECT and an arithmetic expression.
Another winner seed carries a CEXT keyword-like token fragment.
Loser-blocking seeds are punctuation/whitespace-heavy non-token byte runs.
At the blocker source line the winner takes the true side while the loser never does.
Only the winner reaches the expression-resolution walkers, which the loser invokes zero times.
value_profile keys a CMP_MAP by operand-distance bucket and preserves inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The distance bucket encodes Hamming distance or matching-prefix length, i.e. a similarity gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were (Hamming distance bucket for `trace_cmp*`; matching-prefix
length for string/memory CMPs).

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only per-edge hit-count feedback and no CMP distance gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile vs naive), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory db_query.py lineage dash-row check does not apply; mechanism is supported by the value_profile CMP_MAP prefix-distance feedback described in MECHANISM CONTEXT plus the winner-only reach into expression-resolution code.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gated comparison is a single byte equality with no near-miss distance structure (no partial-prefix credit) yet value_profile still resolves it far faster than naive — showing the gradient is not what enables the winner here.

weakest_evidence_point: The winner/loser seeds are stored by value_profile/naive respectively but byte-diff offsets are scattered, so the exact compared token/character-class at 159623 is inferred from surrounding SQL parsing context rather than pinned to a specific operand literal.
